hero_sub: A LAN-first campus emergency system built on Asterisk. The whole campus dials a single number to reach a trained response team — with no internet, no cloud, and no SIM.
features_sub: Nineteen features, distilled into what matters when seconds count.
f1_desc: The whole campus dials 111. Calls land in the response-team queue and are dispatched to the right people.
f2_desc: Trained responder desks answer, transfer, and escalate. Unanswered emergencies trigger an automatic callback.
f3_desc: When no responder is free, an automatic guide walks the caller through first aid — recorded from the first second.
f4_desc: Per-caller voice routing plays prompts in each person's own language, generated offline with neural text-to-speech.
f5_desc: A single operations dashboard and always-on LED wallboards show the queue, calls, and status in real time.
f6_desc: Runs entirely on the local network. No emergency audio or data ever leaves the building.
arch_sub: From a caller in distress to the right responder — every path stays on the campus network.
demo_sub: A short walkthrough — from a caller dialing 111 to the response team answering on the live operations console.
lang_sub: Every prompt, the console, and the wallboards localize to the caller's language — fully offline.
docs_sub: Architecture, runbooks, feature specs, and deployment guides — all in one place.
footer_tagline: Campus emergency communication, built to work when everything else fails.
hpe_sub: The backbone of UPES-ECS is enterprise networking infrastructure from HPE — engineered for high availability, low latency, and complete local network control, with no dependence on the internet or cellular networks.
hpe_srx_desc: Firewall, DHCP, and local time source in one branch gateway. It enforces the LAN-only boundary, so emergency traffic never leaves campus.
hpe_ex_desc: Fanless PoE switches power and connect every phone, access point, and server. They carry the voice network, give 111 priority with quality-of-service, and can pinpoint a caller by switch port.
hpe_ap_desc: Wi-Fi 6 access points carry the softphones that dial 111 — reaching every corner of campus, entirely on the local network.
hpe_note: HPE Juniper Networking hardware — presented at HPE's Bangalore University Networking Day.
collab_sub: In July 2026, UPES-ECS was presented alongside HPE's enterprise networking team — first at HPE's campus in Bengaluru, then live at UPES.
collab_1_desc: 15 July 2026 · HPE Mahadevapura Campus, Bengaluru — a first-hand look at enterprise networking and data-center infrastructure at scale.
collab_2_desc: 20 July 2026 · UPES — UPES-ECS demonstrated live on its HPE- and Juniper-powered networking backbone, end to end.
team_sub: UPES-ECS was designed, built, and presented by two students at UPES.
res_sub: No internet. No cellular. No cloud. UPES-ECS runs entirely on the campus network, so the one number that matters keeps working when everything else goes down.
res_ha_d: Redundant, self-healing network paths keep the hotline reachable.
res_ll_d: Local switching connects every emergency call in an instant.
res_lc_d: Every packet stays on campus, fully under your control.
scale_sub: The same HPE-powered design scales past a single campus — to universities, disaster-response sites, and any critical-communication zone where reliability is essential.
